U1.title: Log family at ratio -1/36 numerically equals L- Log family limit (2/log(2))
U1.next_action: FLAG_FOR_SYNTHESIZER -- recommend a follow-up Mobius-equivalence check between the two PCFs before treating as a counterexample. If equivalent, the preprint claim is unaffected (just a representative-class artifact). If genuinely distinct, this is the first off-locus Log family at b1=6 and warrants amendment of the preprint v2.
U1.preprint_impact: MILD -- preprint (DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19783312) claimed F(2,5) confirms 56 Trans and 12 Log families, all with the same ratio [-2/9]'. A Log family at ratio -1/36 is technically a counterexample to the ll Log at -2/9' formulation, IF it is a genuinely-distinct family rather than an equivalence-collapse of the L- Log family.
U1.interpretation: Same constant 2/log(2) as the L-/Log hit (-8, 0, 0, 6, 4) at ratio -2/9. Either a numerical coincidence or an underlying Mobius-equivalence between (-1, 0, 0, 6, 3) and (-8, 0, 0, 6, 4).

U3.interpretation: Sign-flip mate of the b1=6 L+ hit (9, 0, 0, 6, 3) at L = +12/pi. Confirms the structural symmetry a_n(-c) = (-1)^n * a_n(+c) (017f STRUCTURAL FINDING from 2026-05-03) propagates through the limit at b1=-6: L(-c) = -L(+c) for this Brouncker-boundary family.

Brings b1=6 L- count to 3 + the 2 yesterday-hits at (b0=2 family) = a more complete Trans-stratum-proper roster on L-.
